title: Magecart Skimmer Injected into Shopify Payment Checkout — 12 Retailers Compromised
content: Threat intelligence firm has identified an active Magecart campaign targeting Shopify Plus storefronts through a compromised third-party review widget. The skimmer harvests payment card data and billing addresses in real-time during checkout and exfiltrates them to attacker-controlled domains. IoCs include injected JavaScript from CDN domain reviewwidget-cdn[.]net. MITRE ATT&CK T1185 (Browser Session Hijacking) and T1059.007 (JavaScript) apply. All Shopify Plus merchants using third-party review apps should audit active scripts immediately.

title: Stripe Webhook Endpoint Exploitation Enables Fraudulent Refund Processing
content: Security researchers have published a technique exploiting improperly validated Stripe webhook endpoints to trigger fraudulent refund events without a valid Stripe signature. Merchants with unvalidated webhook handlers are vulnerable to replay attacks and fraudulent credit issuances. CVSS score estimated at 9.1. The attack requires no authentication and exploits CWE-345 (Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity). Retailers must implement Stripe-Signature header validation immediately.

title: Azure AD Conditional Access Policy Misconfiguration Exposes Retail Admin Portals
content: Microsoft has issued a security advisory regarding misconfigured Conditional Access policies in Azure AD that inadvertently allow legacy authentication protocols (SMTP, IMAP, POP3) to bypass MFA requirements. Retail organisations using Azure-hosted ERP and e-commerce admin portals with legacy protocol support enabled are at risk of password spray attacks. CVE-2024-29140 scores CVSS 7.5. Recommended mitigation: enable block legacy authentication policy in all conditional access rules.
